items.title: India's Russian Crude Imports Jump 90% in March After U.S. Waiver
items.summary: India's imports of Russian crude oil jumped by 90% in March versus February, following the major supply disruption in the Middle East and the U.S. waiver of purchases of Russian crude already loaded on tankers. Following subdued purchases between Nov

items.title: UAE's Biggest Gas Plant Forced Offline for Second Time Since War Began
items.summary: Operations at the Habshan gas facilities, the biggest gas processing site in the United Arab Emirates, were suspended early on Friday following a fire that erupted after an attack, Abu Dhabi Media Office said. The Habshan onshore facilities are part

items.title: India Passes on Iranian Oil as Sanctioned Tanker Redirects to China
items.summary: A sanctioned tanker carrying Iranian crude has abruptly changed destination from a port on India's west coast to an import terminal in China, in a move suggesting that India would not be imminently importing its first crude from Iran in seven years.

items.title: Oil Rally Accelerates as Traders Price in Real Supply Disruption
items.summary: May WTI crude oil surged sharply in the holiday-shortened week ending April 3, settling at $111.54, up $11.90 or +11.94%. The move was driven by a combination of geopolitical escalation, supply disruption risks, and growing concerns about global oil
